summary: Decompiled and cleaned up src/melee/mp/mpisland.c, mainly replacing pseudo-C in mpIsland_8005A728 with typed collision/map traversal code and adding a substantial C implementation of mpIsland_8005B004. The PR also adjusted static data placement for mpIsland_804D8158 and included baselib/debug.h for HSD_ASSERT. Decomp.dev reported mpisland data sections reaching 100%, mpIsland_8005ACE8 reaching 100%, mpIsland_8005A728 improving from 53.62% to 90.36%, and mpIsland_8005B004 improving from 0% to 94.81%. Human PR text was only 'tracking PR' and there were no review comments, so intent is inferred mostly from the diff and bot match report.
what_changed: Added #include <baselib/debug.h> so mpisland.c can use HSD_ASSERT instead of raw __assert calls.
Moved/declared static float mpIsland_804D8158 near the other mpIsland globals, with address comment /* 4D8158 */, contributing to sdata/sdata2 matching.
Rewrote mpIsland_8005A728 from register-style pseudo-C into typed C using MapCollData, CollLine, CollVtx, mp_UnkStruct0, named counters, visited[0x600], and PAD_STACK(0x10).
mpIsland_8005A728 now explicitly processes floor segments using map->floor_count/map->floor_start and line x0->next_id0 links gated by CollLine_Floor.
mpIsland_8005A728 now explicitly processes ceiling segments using map->ceiling_count/map->ceiling_start and line x0->prev_id0 links gated by CollLine_Ceiling.

Both floor and ceiling segment builders allocate 0x2C-byte mp_UnkStruct0 nodes, assert allocation success, fill endpoints, vertex positions, z values from mpIsland_804D8158, x20 hidden/enabled state, and x28 via mpJointFromLine or joint index.
Small declaration-order adjustment in mpIsland_8005ACE8 moved CollJoint* temp_r3 above later locals and decomp.dev reported that function reaching 100%.
Implemented mpIsland_8005B004 in C, replacing the prior /// #mpIsland_8005B004 placeholder.

mpIsland_8005B004 reuses existing mp_UnkStruct0 nodes from one list to another for a target joint, scans dynamic collision lines inside a CollJoint/MapJoint range, tracks visited lines, detects loops, and allocates/reuses segment nodes for matching line flag groups.
Decomp.dev bot reported +3644 matched code bytes and +48 matched data bytes overall for GALE01, including mpisland .sdata and .sdata2 reaching 100%.
smart_moves: Converted byte/offset-heavy pseudo-C into struct-field reads such as map->floor_count, lines[end_idx].x0->next_id0, lines[end_idx].x0->hi_flags, and vtx[mpisp->x4].pos.x, making the traversal logic reviewable without losing matching-sensitive details.
Kept explicit allocation size 0x2C and HSD_ASSERT line immediates 62 and 0x3E, which preserves evidence from the original assert sites while improving source readability.
Used a fixed u8 visited[0x600] buffer plus PAD_STACK to preserve stack shape while naming the algorithm as a visited-line scan.

Separated floor and ceiling processing into mirrored blocks but preserved their directional differences: floors follow next_id0 and use v0_idx to v1_idx, ceilings follow prev_id0 and reverse the vertex endpoints.
Used symbolic collision constants where available, including LINE_FLAG_ENABLED, LINE_FLAG_HIDDEN, CollLine_Floor, and CollLine_Ceiling, instead of raw magic flag tests.

For mpIsland_8005B004, preserved a matching-oriented goto label after_alloc and narrow local scopes around loop variables, suggesting source was shaped around compiler output rather than only high-level style.
Recycled nodes from *arg1 before allocating new mp_UnkStruct0 nodes in mpIsland_8005B004, matching the apparent original list-management behavior.
decomp_lessons: For map/collision decomp, replacing pointer arithmetic with known structs can unlock large match improvements while simultaneously documenting traversal semantics.

When a function walks linked collision lines, keep the exact traversal direction explicit: floor chains used next_id0, ceiling chains used prev_id0, and dynamic scans in mpIsland_8005B004 use both directions depending on loop detection.
A visited bitmap is useful both algorithmically and for matching; preserving its exact size, zeroing call, and skip-loop shape can matter.
Static data placement can be part of the match. Moving mpIsland_804D8158 to the global declaration area with an address comment coincided with mpisland sdata/sdata2 gains.
Use project macros like HSD_ASSERT when the original code asserted allocation success; include baselib/debug.h rather than open-coding __assert.
Partial decomp is still valuable: mpIsland_8005B004 went from 0% to 94.81% even though it did not fully match.
Declaration order can affect matching in near-matched functions, as shown by the small mpIsland_8005ACE8 local declaration reorder associated with a 99.81% to 100% report.
Preserve original control-flow artifacts such as local blocks, break conditions, and goto labels when needed to maintain compiler-compatible codegen.
naming_conventions: Address-named globals follow the existing style: mpIsland_804D8158 and mpIsland_80458E88, with address comments such as /* 4D8158 */ and /* 458E88 */.
Function names remain address-based mpIsland symbols, such as mpIsland_8005A728, mpIsland_8005ACE8, mpIsland_8005B004, and mpIsland_8005B334.
Typed locals use short semantic names: map, lines, vtx, joints, inner, mpisp, prev, next, count, line_idx, end_idx, start_idx, hidden, visited, z_val.
Collision flags and line classes are represented with symbolic constants where known: LINE_FLAG_ENABLED, LINE_FLAG_HIDDEN, CollLine_Floor, CollLine_Ceiling.
mp_UnkStruct0 fields remain partially unknown/x-prefixed, such as next, x4, x6, x8, x14, x20, x24, x26, and x28, rather than inventing unsupported semantic names.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Added PAD_STACK(0x10) / PAD_STACK(16) alongside u8 visited[0x600] to preserve stack layout.
Used HSD_ASSERT with explicit source line constants, including 62, 0x3E, and 0x206.
Kept exact HSD_MemAlloc(0x2C) allocation size for mp_UnkStruct0 nodes.
Used memzero(visited, 0x600u) rather than a higher-level initializer.
Preserved matching-sensitive skip loops over visited entries with pointer p and counter ctr inside inner scopes.
Used type_flag = arg3 | 0x10 and line flag masking with (lines[line_idx].flags & 0x1F) in mpIsland_8005B004.
Retained a goto after_alloc path in mpIsland_8005B004 around loop/assert handling.
Filled Vec3 components field-by-field from CollVtx positions and mpIsland_804D8158 rather than using aggregate assignment.
Used s16 link/next variables for line linkage values, matching the signed -1 sentinel checks.
review_feedback: No human review comments were present in the provided slice.
The PR body contained only 'tracking PR', so no detailed author rationale was available.
Decomp.dev bot reported GALE01 matched code improved by +0.09% (+3644 bytes) and matched data by +48 bytes.

Bot report listed mpisland .sdata2 from 50.00% to 100.00%, mpisland .sdata from 60.00% to 100.00%, mpIsland_8005ACE8 from 99.81% to 100.00%, mpIsland_8005A728 from 53.62% to 90.36%, and mpIsland_8005B004 from 0.00% to 94.81%.
follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that fully match mpIsland_8005B004 after this 94.81% implementation.
Search for mp_UnkStruct0 field naming improvements around x20, x24, x26, and x28.
Look for other mp collision PRs that use visited[0x600] and PAD_STACK to match traversal functions.
Check whether mpIsland_804D8158 gained a semantic name in later map/collision work.
Compare floor and ceiling line traversal patterns in other mp modules using next_id0, prev_id0, CollLine_Floor, and CollLine_Ceiling.
